zh.lifeSummary: 當John de La Bere於1295年出生在英國賀福爾時，他的父親Richard de La Bere24歲，母親Sybil Chabbonare24歲。他和Agnes de Turberville至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1366年在英國英格蘭可瓦爾享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭可瓦爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Bere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Beer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erhard, Hans, Heinz, Aloys, Bodo, Erna, Ernst, Frieda, Friedhelm, Hanni.

English (West Country): habitational name from any of the forty or so places in southwestern England called Beer(e) or Bear(e). Most of these derive their names from the West Saxon dative case, beara, of Old English bearu ‘grove, wood’ (the standard Old English dative bearwe being preserved in Barrow ). In some cases the surname may be topographic in origin, from atte beare ‘at the grove’. Some may be from Middle English bere ‘woodland swine-pasture’ (Old English bǣr). Compare Bear 1.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de La Bere was born in 1295, in Herefordshire, England, his father, Richard de La Bere, was 24 and his mother, Sybil Chabbonare, was 24. He had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Agnes de Turberville. He died in 1366, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de La Bere est né en 1295, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de La Bere, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Sybil Chabbonare, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Agnes de Turberville. Il est décédé en 1366, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de La Bere 1295 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de La Bere 24 und seine Mutter Sybil Chabbonare 24. Er hatte mit Agnes de Turberville mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1366 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de La Bere è nato il nel 1295 nel Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Richard de La Bere, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Sybil Chabbonare, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Agnes de Turberville. Morì nel 1366, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John de La Bereが1295年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard de La Bereは24歳，母Sybil Chabbonareは24歳であった。彼はAgnes de Turbervilleとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1366年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスコルンワルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de La Bere(이)가 1295년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de La Bere(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Sybil Chabbonare(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Agnes de Turberville(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1366년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de La Bere nasceu em 1295, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de La Bere, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Sybil Chabbonare, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Agnes de Turberville. Ele faleceu em 1366, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de La Bere родился в 1295 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de La Bere было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Sybil Chabbonare, 24. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Agnes de Turberville. Он умер в 1366 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de La Bere nació en 1295, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de La Bere, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Sybil Chabbonare, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas con Agnes de Turberville. Murió en 1366, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
